message:
chore(gitignore): ignore beads write-lock and e2e log files

These two patterns kept showing up as untracked in agent sessions:

- \`.beads/.write.lock\` — per-process advisory lock written by \`br\`
  on every invocation. Already cleaned up automatically; no value in
  ever committing it.
- \`e2e_*.log\` — output captured by the destructive-equivalents e2e
  harness when run locally. Each run regenerates the file; previous
  runs' output is not interesting in git history.

No code change, just suppresses recurring noise in \`git status\`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -75,3 +75,9 @@ hello_write.txt
 
 # Agent mail project identity
 .agent-mail-project-id
+
+# Beads write lock (per-process, regenerated on each br invocation)
+.beads/.write.lock
+
+# E2E test run logs
+e2e_*.log
